positioning: Ouro Praxis is the only premium, concept-led brand in the AI database-reactivation niche. Where every competitor runs the same GoHighLevel script behind loud, dollar-first, screenshot-wall landing pages, Ouro Praxis owns the trust-and-taste high ground the category has vacated. Positioning statement: "Ouro Praxis is the AI reactivation partner for local service businesses that refuse to accept a lead is ever truly dead. We revive the dormant leads already sitting in your CRM into booked calls and recovered revenue, on a recurring cycle, in your brand voice, without burning your list or your reputation. Not new acquisition. Transmutation of what you already own." The brand is built on the ouroboros, the serpent that eats its own tail: the eternal cycle of death and rebirth, where the end of a lead feeds the beginning of a sale. Strategic payload of the concept: it reframes reactivation from a one-off cash grab into a dignified, repeatable cycle, which is exactly what justifies recurring retainer pricing where competitors sell single blasts. Three moats competitors cannot copy on a 90-minute launch template: (1) compliance and sender-reputation stewardship as a visible pillar, (2) brand-voice fidelity so the AI sounds like the client and not a bot, (3) an Awwwards-caliber pastel-minimalist brand that signals premium before a word is read.
offer: One flagship productized offer, "The Ouroboros Engine," a done-for-you reactivation cycle sold in three named phases. EXHUME: we audit, clean, dedupe, segment and compliance-scrub your dormant database to surface the buried buyers. REVIVE: a custom AI SMS agent trained on your brand voice reopens the conversation, answers questions, handles objections and qualifies intent in natural human-paced texting. RETURN: qualified prospects land on your calendar as booked appointments, the tail feeding the head. Praxis is the disciplined end-to-end execution of that cycle, run entirely for you. Value stack, each item anchored to a real cost the owner recognizes: full database exhumation and hygiene (the work of a data analyst); a custom-trained AI SMS agent in your brand voice, never a template (the output of a full-time SDR); conversation scripts engineered from campaigns that hit a 41% reply rate against an 8% industry average; done-for-you CRM and calendar integration, live in 7 days; a human handoff protocol so hot leads reach your team the moment they are ready to buy; weekly reactivation reporting on conversations, replies, booked calls and recovered revenue; optional add-on Engines for Speed-to-Lead (sub-60-second new-lead contact), Out-of-Hours booking, and Google Reviews auto-reply. Pricing architecture: a refundable activation fee (guide 2,000 to 3,000 GBP) plus a performance component (per booked appointment or a revenue share), never presented as a cost but always as a fraction of the recoverable revenue the client's own calculator surfaces. Honest scarcity, not fake timers: founder-built Engines cap monthly onboards, and one Engine per niche per market locks a competitor out for as long as the client stays. "You bring the list. We bring it back to life. One client per niche, per market."
guarantee: The Rebirth Guarantee (recommended, a hybrid conditional plus performance structure that fully reverses buyer risk while protecting Ouro Praxis from bad inputs). Copy: "Your setup fee is 100% refundable if the Ouroboros Engine does not book at least [X] qualified appointments in your first 30 days. After that, you only ever pay for results we produce. We take the risk of reviving your list. You just take the calls." Conditions that keep it credible and filter tire-kickers, stated plainly: a minimum viable list size, the client must take the booked calls, and brand voice must be approved within 48 hours of onboarding. This is the strongest position on the Perceived-Likelihood axis of the value equation because it removes both entry risk (refundable activation) and ongoing risk (pay for results), while the conditions signal a real system rather than a gamble. Two alternates held in reserve for different prospects: a pure pay-on-results version for previously-burned skeptics ("a small activation fee, then only for qualified appointments we actually book"), and an ROI-multiple work-free version for confident high-AOV clients ("at least 3x your investment in recovered revenue within 90 days or we keep running the Engine free until you hit it").
narrativeSpine: THE FRAME (hero): The end of a lead is the beginning of a sale. Open on the ouroboros, the eternal cycle. Dead is a label the owner assigned, not a fact. Reframe the CRM from graveyard to cycle before making a single claim.

THE DORMANT (problem / nigredo, the blackening): Name the owner's real, guilt-laced reality. 71 to 73% of leads never worked, 47-hour average response times, thousands of contacts doing nothing. This is stored energy, not failure. Money already spent, sitting dead.

THE CYCLE (mechanism / how it works): The Ouroboros Engine in three alchemical phases, Exhume then Revive then Return, mapped to nigredo (the dead list), albedo (AI SMS revives and qualifies at dawn), rubedo (booked calls, the gold). The metaphor does the explaining.
THE FOUR SERPENTS (products): Four bots presented as four segments of one closing loop, each a coil of the same serpent. Reactivation, Speed-to-Lead, Out-of-Hours, Reviews.

THE RETURN (proof / metrics): The numbers a dead list produces once it is alive again. 41% vs 8% shown as a ratio, £500k+ recovered, 200k+ leads returned, 536% conversion lift, presented as a quiet premium data row plus animated counters, never a screenshot wall.
THE TRANSMUTATION ENGINE (calculator): Feed in base matter (list size, AOV), watch it resolve to gold (recoverable revenue). The number closes the loop as it settles.
THE VOICES (testimonials): Four verticalized owner voices, each carrying one earned proof point, each front-loading the death so the rebirth lands.

THE ETERNAL RETURN (offer + guarantee + objections): The Ouroboros Engine, the Rebirth Guarantee, and the trust pillar competitors lack (no burning your list). Recurring cycle framing justifies the retainer. Amor fati: want the return.
PRAXIS (final CTA): From philosophy to action. The cycle is the idea. Praxis is where it pays. Begin the cycle.
voiceGuide: Register: calm premium authority. The confident quiet of a brand that does not need to shout because the concept and the numbers do the work. Think a private bank or a heritage watchmaker that happens to run AI, not a conversion-bro funnel.

Theme discipline: death is always the doorway, never the dwelling. USE cycle, return, revive, renew, reawaken, dawn, gold, transmute, dormant, closed loop, second life, stored energy. HARD-AVOID corpse, grave, tomb, necromancy, zombie, skulls, goth or black-metal styling, and literal resurrection-of-the-dead imagery. One tasteful exception: the word graveyard may appear in an owner's own quoted voice or a single problem-section line, because owners genuinely say 'my CRM is a graveyard.' Never in headlines.
Reframe, do not gore. 'Dormant, not dead.' 'The conversation died, the lead never did.' Rebirth is carried by meaning, not morbidity.

Escape the category cliche. Never lead with a dollar sign or a '$10K in 48 hours' hype headline, that is exactly where every competitor lives. Lead with the concept and the reframe; let the numbers arrive as quiet proof.

Every claim traces to a real proof point: 41% reply vs 8% industry, £500k+ generated, 200k+ leads reactivated, 536% conversion lift, 10+ businesses, £40k to £100k recovered per campaign, <60s speed-to-lead. Invent nothing beyond these.
Sell the two things the owner actually wants: certainty and zero lift. 'You bring the list, we bring it back to life.' 'You just take the calls.' Reclaimed time as much as reclaimed money.

Mirror owner words in headlines and emotional copy (leads just sitting there, money you already spent, ghosted quotes, they went with whoever answered first). Use vendor words in functional labels (reactivation, reply rate, close rate, AOV, booked appointments).

Mechanics: NO em-dashes anywhere; use commas, periods, or parentheses. NO banned filler verbs (unlock, leverage, empower, supercharge, revolutionize, seamless, elevate, harness, unleash, delve). Prefer short declarative sentences. Contractions are fine and warm.

Naming: OURO (the cycle, ouroboros) plus PRAXIS (the doing). 'The cycle is the philosophy. Praxis is where it pays.' Capitalize product and phase names (The Ouroboros Engine, Exhume, Revive, Return) to make them read as proprietary IP.

Typographic restraint matches verbal restraint: sparse copy, generous whitespace, the pastel gold-thread ouroboros faint behind content. Let the counters and the calculator be the loudest thing on the page, and even they settle quietly rather than spin.

hero.headline: The end of a lead is the beginning of a sale.
headlineAlts: Your CRM is not a graveyard. It's a cycle.
hero.subhead: AI-led SMS conversations that revive the dormant leads already sitting in your CRM into booked calls and recovered revenue. No new ad spend. No new staff. No lifting a finger.
hero.supportingLine: The revenue was never gone. It was waiting to be reawakened.
hero.ctaSecondary: See what your list is worth
hero.animationNote: A single thread departs as a cold grey line and returns to complete the circle as it warms to gold. The closing of the loop is the value proposition, rendered.

products.sectionSub: Each bot is a segment of the same cycle, always on, revenue where there was silence.

items.name: The Revival
items.body: Our AI texts your dormant list in your brand voice, reopens real conversations, and books the buyers hiding in leads you already paid to acquire. We have reawakened more than 200,000 of them.

items.name: First Response
items.body: Every new lead gets a human-quality reply in under 60 seconds, day or night. Responding inside a minute triples close rates and beats the 47-hour industry average that hands your leads to whoever answered first.

items.name: The Night Shift
items.body: Half your leads arrive when you are closed. This one never closes. It qualifies and books across evenings, weekends and holidays, so a dark office never costs you another sale.

items.name: The Chorus
items.body: It replies to every review in your brand voice within minutes, protecting your rating and compounding your local reputation while you get on with the work.

proof.sectionHeadline: The numbers a dead list produces once it's alive again.
proof.sectionSub: Quiet proof, not a screenshot wall.

metrics.label: average reply rate
metrics.note: Shown as a paired bar, 41 counting up beside a ghosted 8. A roughly 5x multiple, instantly legible.

metrics.label: recovered for clients
metrics.note: Currency prefix, plus sign settles in after the count.

metrics.label: leads reactivated
metrics.note: Fastest count of the group, reinforcing scale.

metrics.label: increase in lead conversions
metrics.note: Largest tile, given the most weight.

proof.animationSpec: Trigger on scroll at ~40% visibility, fire once. The ouroboros stroke draws a full circle over ~1.8s while each number counts up on an ease-out cubic and settles, never a linear slot-machine spin. Tabular figures so digits do not jitter. prefers-reduced-motion renders final values instantly with no draw.
proof.caseCardStructure: Front-load the death so the rebirth lands. Fields: [Vertical tag] · [The Graveyard: list size + age + prior status] · [Headline metric, large] · [Supporting stat] · [Window] · [Product used] · [Pull-quote + name/role/business] · [Micro-CTA: See what your dead list is worth].

caseCardExample.headline: $58K recovered
caseCardExample.quote: It paid for itself before we'd finished onboarding.
caseCardExample.attribution: Dr. Marissa Cole, Brightwater Family Dental
caseCardExample.cta: See what your dead list is worth

calculator.title: How much revenue is dead in your database right now?
calculator.sub: Feed in the base matter. Watch it resolve to gold.

inputLabels.leads: Leads sitting cold in your CRM

calculator.resultLine: You could recover about £[X] from leads you already paid for, without spending a penny on new ads.
calculator.anchorMicrocopy: Reactivating an old lead costs 5 to 7 times less than buying a new one. Most of this revenue is just sitting there.
calculator.proofChip: 41% avg reply rate vs 8% industry · £500K+ recovered for clients · 200K+ leads revived.
calculator.resultAnimationNote: The recovered number counts up and the ouroboros ring closes as it settles, gold completing the loop.

items.quote: We had 3,800 old quote enquiries just rotting in the CRM, people who ghosted us months ago. Ouro Praxis switched their bot on and within a fortnight it was holding real conversations with 4 out of 10 of them. Four out of ten. Our own follow-up never cracked one in ten. We booked 31 surveys off a list I'd already written off as dead.
items.name: Gareth Whitfield

items.quote: These were lapsed patients, hygiene recalls we'd chased twice and given up on. The AI texted them in our practice's voice, handled the back-and-forth, and dropped booked appointments straight onto our schedule. First campaign brought back just under $58,000 in treatment and recall revenue from patients we assumed were gone for good. It paid for itself before we'd finished onboarding.
items.name: Dr. Marissa Cole
items.role: Practice Owner, Brightwater Family Dental, Austin, TX

items.quote: Our lead list was a graveyard, trial sign-ups and enquiries going back over a year that never converted. I was sceptical an SMS bot could do anything with them. It reactivated the dead ones and our conversions off that segment went up more than 5x versus when my front desk was chasing them by hand. Members walking in quoting a text they got at 9pm on a Sunday. That's the bit that sold me.
items.name: Danny Osei

items.quote: We're a small crew, nobody here has time to work an old lead list. Ouro Praxis reactivated thousands of aged quotes for us without us hiring a single person. The bot qualified them, booked the good ones, and filtered the tyre-kickers. It's like a full-time appointment setter that never sleeps and never quits. Best part: the leads I'd literally exported to a 'do not bother' spreadsheet are the ones now paying our bills.
items.name: Rick Tannenbaum
items.role: GM, ClearView Windows & Exteriors, Columbus, OH

items.q: Won't texting old leads annoy them or damage my brand?
items.a: No blasting. Every message references their original enquiry, opens in your brand voice, leads with something useful rather than a hard pitch, and honours every opt-out instantly. It reads like a helpful check-in, one offer, one question, one next step. Our 41% average reply rate tells us people are glad to hear from you.

items.q: Is this compliant? I don't want my number blacklisted.
items.a: Protecting your sender reputation is our job, not your homework. We handle A2P registration, scrub against reassigned-number databases, honour every opt-out instantly, and keep STOP rates well under carrier limits with compliant messaging. We reactivate without ever burning your list, because your number reputation is the asset.

items.q: My leads are basically dead. Is there really anything left?
items.a: Dead is a label you assigned, not a fact. Those people had a real need and their timing simply changed. We routinely turn lists owners had written off to zero into tens of thousands in booked revenue. We have reawakened over 200,000 leads. Dormant is not dead.

items.q: Won't an AI bot sound robotic and make my business look bad?
items.a: It is trained on your brand voice, not a template, and texts like a real person. The moment a lead is genuinely interested it hands the conversation to your team. It never fumbles a hot lead. It just makes sure one finally gets worked.

items.q: Do I need to hire anyone or change my systems?
items.a: You don't touch it. The Engine works your entire database on its own and only pings your team when someone is ready to book. It runs alongside the CRM you already use, and you are live in 7 days. Zero extra headcount.

items.q: How quickly do I see results?
items.a: Reactivation is the fastest revenue you own, because these people already know you. Replies land within days and booked calls in the first week. Most clients recover £40,000 to £100,000 from a single campaign, and across clients this has driven a 536% increase in conversions and over £500,000 in generated revenue.

founder.name: Everett
founder.title: Founder, Ouro Praxis
founder.paragraph: Everett founded Ouro Praxis on one refusal: to accept that a lead is ever truly dead. The most valuable asset a business owns is the list it has stopped calling, every dormant contact money already spent to earn attention that was never converted. He built Ouro Praxis to close that gap with AI that texts like a person, listens like a closer, and books like clockwork, always in the client's own voice and never at the cost of the client's reputation. The name is deliberate. Ouroboros for the cycle that has no end. Praxis for the work that makes it real. The cycle is the philosophy. Praxis is where it pays. Under his direction the company has reawakened more than 200,000 leads and returned over £500,000 to businesses that had written that revenue off for good.

finalCta.headline: Close the loop. Recover the revenue.
finalCta.sub: The riskiest thing you can do with your database is nothing. It loses value every day it sits. Bring us the list you gave up on. We will hand it back as booked calls.
finalCta.guaranteeReminder: The Rebirth Guarantee: if your first 30-day cycle doesn't book at least [X] qualified appointments, your setup fee is refunded. After that you only ever pay for results.
finalCta.scarcityLine: Founder-built Engines, a limited number of onboards each month. One Engine per niche, per market. Once your competitor is in, you're locked out.

footer.descriptor: AI database reactivation for local service businesses. We revive the dormant leads you already own into booked calls and recovered revenue.
footer.closingMotto: The cycle is the philosophy. Praxis is where it pays.
footer.legalLine: We reactivate without burning your list. Your sender reputation is the asset.
footer.copyright: © Ouro Praxis. The end of a lead is the beginning of a sale.
